IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript:
This work is taking place on Buckshorn Lane
in Eye. A road closure will be in place to allow
the work to be carried out safely. Access to
your property will always be provided.
To provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, we carry out regular inspections of our
equipment in your area. This has identified a pole that needs replacing and upgrading. We will also
be installing some equipment on the new pole to ensure the cables are at statutory height. Doing
this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future.To complete the work safely, we need to turn your power off to upgrade the pole and install the
new equipment. To ensure the power is off for the shortest possible time, our engineers may carry
out preparation works prior to the power cut. We will need access to the inside of your property to
conduct safety checks in the morning before we switch off the power and we will need to do
another check when we turn the power back on.

This work is taking place near River Chelmer along Thaxted Road and Walden Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: More homes and businesses adopt low-carbon technologies such as electric vehicles heat pumps and renewable generation, energy demand on the local network is changing. We are changing the voltage settings at the transformer to help provide additional capacity for low carbon technologies, improve efficiency and reduce energy losses and help support a cleaner and more sustainable energy network. This will help the needs for net zero now and in the future. Whilst carrying out this work we will also be trimming trees that are close to the overhead cables, completing this work alongside to reduce there being further planned power cuts. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely cut the trees away from the cables and change the voltage setting at the transformer. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted by unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months.This work is taking place on West End Goudhurst Road.

Framfield Road in Blackboys.We regularly inspect our electrical equipment to ensure it is safe and efficient. We need to examine
a transformer on our electricity poles to check it is functioning properly. This essential work helps
to provide a reliable electricity supply to your property, reducing the risk of any unplanned power
cuts in the future.
To keep everyone safe while we carry out these works, we will need to temporarily turn off your
electricity. We are taking extra measures to limit the time your power is off and we'll do our best to
cause as little disruption as possible. Doing this will improve the reliability of the electricity network
in the future as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last
6 months.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: This is the planned power cut that was originally scheduled for the 22nd of January 2026. A customer has asked UK Power Networks to switch off the power so they can safely cut back trees in the area that have grown close to overhead electricity cables. These trees could cause damage to the cables and affect your electricity supply. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so a tree surgeon can safely cut the trees away from 3 sections of cables. Doing this will reduce the risk of you having an unplanned power cut in the future as we appreciate some customers may have experienced unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near New Road. Multi way traffic lights will be in place on the junction of New Road, Rectory Road and Scrub Lane to allow the works to be carried out safely. Access to your property will always be provided.

Steps.Message: Hi, I'm Steph and I'm the manager of the text messaging team at UK Power Networks. Today's power cut was an unexpected fault on the overhead electrical equipment that affected 2051 properties, so I'm sorry that we weren't able to provide you with prior notice. I'm pleased to report our staff have resolved this by removing the tree that had fallen onto our overhead electrical cables to get your lights back on as soon as possible. Were your text messages delayed during this fault? Find out why here https://UKPN.short.gy/no-sms I personally apologise for the disruption this caused you. We aim to provide a 10/10 service in keeping you updated, so if you'd like to talk to us about anything or are still without power, please call us on 105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 23:26 - Our engineers have restored power to all 2051 properties in the PE32 and PE37 area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have removed the tree that had fallen onto the overhead electrical cables.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Sent by David at 23:24 - We're sorry for the disruption to your power today, especially for those who may be more vulnerable without the electric. If you're worried about an elderly or ill person in your property who is affected by the power cut today, we are able to provide some support. For more information just click here: https://UKPN.short.gy/PSR If you have any further concerns or questions though, don't hesitate to call us free on 08003163105. We'll continue to keep you updated with our engineer's progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.

Steps.Message:
We're sorry your power has gone off again.

We believe there may be an underlying issue causing the recent power cuts, but due to the nature of the electricity network, the heat created by a developing fault can cause a break in a cable to reseal itself. This can make it difficult to locate, so while our engineers are testing our equipment thoroughly to pinpoint the issue, this kind of problem may interrupt power multiple times before it's possible for us to fix.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

I'll keep you updated. Sent by David at 22:10
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 00:17 - Hi David here again. We can now confirm that all power in the IG11 7 area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by David at 23:39 - Thanks for bearing with us tonight.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Engineers have arrived on site to investigate what we believe to be an issue at the local substation in the IG11 7 area. Using an average timescale for this type of issue, we're looking to have you back on between 02:30 and 03:30, however we must stress that this time may change, depending on what our staff find. We know that a power cut can be very disruptive; we understand this can greatly impact your daily activities and we want to support you however we can. Steps.Message:
Hi, this is Roland from the messaging team at UK Power Networks. I'm sorry for the power cut today; I know the lights going off can be disruptive.

We know that some customers affected by todays power cut may have had other issues recently, and I appreciate this is frustrating. High voltage cabling can cover a wide area, so sometimes properties very close to you may have had power cuts that you didn't experience.

I know you may not have had any previous power cuts, but if you have, we often get progress updates from our field staff management about investigations into repeat issues, and our Customer Services team will try to contact everyone affected by them to explain what's happening.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Hi there. We've just had a shift change, so Luke, Decasta, Lee and Roland are now here to keep you updated with progress from our field teams.

We know it's frustrating being without power, but we wanted to assure you we're still here and as soon as we have some more information we'll update you again.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 23:56. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
Steps.Name: We are restoring customers automatically
